# Voltura Air

Voltura Air is free Windows software from Voltura AB.

## Summary

Voltura Air turns a phone, tablet, or browser-capable device into a wireless trackpad, keyboard, acknowledged text-transfer tool, dictation surface, and media remote for a Windows 11 PC.

## Links

- Product page: https://voltura.se/air/
- Source code: https://github.com/voltura/voltura-air
- Latest release: https://github.com/voltura/voltura-air/releases/latest
- License: https://github.com/voltura/voltura-air/blob/main/LICENSE
- Security policy: https://github.com/voltura/voltura-air/blob/main/SECURITY.md

## Key facts

- Freeware from Voltura AB.
- Open source under the MIT License.
- Local-network design: no account system, no cloud relay, and no internet input forwarding.
- The Windows host serves the mobile app to trusted devices on the same Wi-Fi or LAN.
- The mobile web app includes Trackpad, Keyboard, Remote, Dictate, Send text to PC, and a tablet split mode that works as a combined couch keyboard and trackpad. Buffered Keyboard sends and text transfer preserve multiline line breaks. The fourth mode button can be Dictation or Send text; text transfer targets the currently focused Windows application, supports an optional final Enter and local snippets, and reports a host-confirmed result.
- Remote mode includes media, volume, YouTube, Kodi, menu navigation, Windows window actions, browser tab/page controls, and host-approved Browser, Spotify, VLC, PowerPoint, or custom executable buttons; 1-10 character button labels are host-managed, and custom paths and arguments stay on the PC and require local approval.
- Remote mode also provides host-permissioned Lock PC, Blackout display, display-off, screen-saver, sign-out, restart, and shut-down controls. Blackout uses a black curtain across all monitors and dismisses on local or remote input without suspending Windows. Screen saver is omitted unless Windows has one enabled and configured. Session-ending actions require hold-to-confirm. Display off also requires confirmation because some PCs enter sleep or Modern Standby, disconnect Voltura Air, and require physical keyboard or mouse input to wake; Windows may then require authentication.
- The Windows tray and Preferences provide Keep awake Off, interval, expiration, indefinite, and Keep screen on controls without editing the selected Windows power plan or requiring elevation. Permissioned mobile devices receive one basic Keep awake toggle that uses the host's screen setting.
- The Windows host can explicitly enable current-user workstation locking without elevation, does not touch automatic sign-in, and offers opt-in sanitized application logging with a themed filterable Diagnostics view.
- Windows release builds are not code-signed, so Windows may show an unknown publisher or SmartScreen warning.

- It detects stale connections and input delivery failures, then shows unavailable/retrying instead of leaving dead controls on screen.
